chunks.text: Ren Ng is a professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at the University of California, Berkeley. His research interests are in imaging, graphics, computer vision, human vision and artificial intelligence. Prior to Berkeley, Ren was founder and CEO of Lytro, Inc., which commercialized his Ph.D. research and brought consumer light field cameras to market. Ren completed his Ph.D. in computer science at Stanford University, and received the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award and Stanford's Arthur Samuel Award.
chunks.question: What is Ren Ng's academic position and research focus?
chunks.answer: Professor at UC Berkeley in imaging and AI.

chunks.text: Ren has received the 2020 ECCV Best Paper Honorable Mention, Jim and Donna Gray Faculty Award for Undergraduate Teaching, Hellman Faculty Fellowship, Sloan Research Fellowship, the HIPA Photographic Research Award, PMDA Technical Achievement Award, R.I.T.'s Imaging Hall of Fame, the Selwyn Award from the Royal Photographic Society, MIT Tech Review's TR35 and Entrepreneur of the Year, Fast Company's 100 Most Creative People in Business, and Silicon Valley Journal's 40 under 40.
chunks.question: What notable awards has Ren Ng received?
chunks.answer: Multiple awards including Sloan Fellowship and TR35.

chunks.text: PhD Students: Hannah Doyle, James Fong, Atsunobu Kotani, Jessica Lee. Alumni: Nicholas Antipa, Grace Kuo, Ben Mildenhall, Pratul Srinivasan, Matthew Tancik, Congli Wang, Xuaner Cecilia Zhang.
chunks.question: Who are some of Ren Ng's PhD students and alumni?
chunks.answer: Students include Hannah Doyle and alumni Ben Mildenhall.

chunks.text: Education: 2006, Ph.D., Computer Science, Stanford University. 2006, M.S., Computer Science, Stanford University. 2001, B.S., Mathematical and Computational Science, Stanford University.
chunks.question: What are Ren Ng's educational qualifications?
chunks.answer: Ph.D., M.S., and B.S. from Stanford University.

chunks.text: Research Areas: Graphics (GR), Signal Processing (SP), Artificial Intelligence (AI). Human Vision; Color Perception; Color Theory; Imaging Systems; Computational Photography; Optics.
chunks.question: What are Ren Ng's primary research areas?
chunks.answer: Graphics, AI, human vision, and computational photography.

chunks.text: Research Centers: Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Visual Computing Lab (VCL).
chunks.question: Which research centers is Ren Ng associated with?
chunks.answer: BAIR and Visual Computing Lab at Berkeley.

chunks.text: Selected Publications include: J. Fong et al., 'Novel color via stimulation of individual photoreceptors at population scale,' Science Advances, 2025. A. Kotani and R. Ng, 'A Computational Framework for Modeling Emergence of Color Vision in the Human Brain,' ICLR 2025. J. Lee et al., 'Theory of Human Tetrachromatic Color Experience and Printing,' ACM TOG, 2024.
chunks.question: What are some recent publications by Ren Ng?
chunks.answer: Papers on color vision and computational frameworks.

chunks.text: Additional publications: H. K. Doyle et al., 'Boosting 2-photon vision with adaptive optics,' Journal of Vision, 2023. V. P. Pandiyan et al., 'Characterizing cone spectral classification by optoretinography,' Biomedical Optics Express, 2022. M. Tancik et al., 'Learned Initializations for Optimizing Coordinate-Based Neural Representations,' CVPR 2021.
chunks.question: What other research has Ren Ng published?
chunks.answer: Works on vision, optics, and neural representations.

chunks.text: Key publications: M. Tancik et al., 'Fourier Features Let Networks Learn High Frequency Functions in Low Dimensional Domains,' NeurIPS 2020. B. Mildenhall et al., 'NeRF: Representing Scenes as Neural Radiance Fields for View Synthesis,' ECCV 2020. M. Tancik et al., 'StegaStamp: Invisible Hyperlinks in Physical Photographs,' CVPR 2020.
chunks.question: What are some influential papers by Ren Ng?
chunks.answer: NeRF, Fourier features, and StegaStamp papers.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: Hellman Fellow (2019), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching (2019), Sloan Research Fellow (2017), MIT Tech Review Top 35 Innovators Under 35 (TR35) (2012), MIT Tech Review Top 10 Breakthrough Technologies (TR10) (2012), ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award (2006).
chunks.question: What specific honors has Ren Ng earned?
chunks.answer: Hellman Fellow, Sloan Fellow, and ACM award.
